metadata.source_paper: Husein, S., Novian, M. I., & Barianto, D. H. (2014). Geological structures and tectonic reconstruction of Luwuk, East Sulawesi. Proceedings of the Indonesian Petroleum Association, 38th Annual Convention & Exhibition (IPA 14-G-137).
metadata.region: East Arm Sulawesi — Luwuk area (Banggai Peninsula)
metadata.comprehension_summary: Field mapping + satellite imagery identifies three structural compartments in Luwuk: (N) extensional block-faulting on Balantak Ophiolite; (C) NW-vergent thin-skinned thrust-fold belt (Salodik Anticlinorium + Batui/Salodik Thrust); (S) gravitational Kemumu rock slides. Revises previous SE-vergent thick-skinned model. Two tectonic pulses: (1) NW compression from North Banda Sea opening → Balantak Fault initiation; (2) Late Pliocene NE push via Sorong Fault activation → basin inversion, Salodik fold-thrust belt formation. Uplift rate 1.84 mm/yr (0.35 Ma terrace).
main_conclusions: Batui/Salodik Thrust is thin-skinned and NW-vergent, not thick-skinned SE-vergent as previously assumed — revises Luwuk thrust-fold belt model
Balantak Fault is the eastern onland continuation of Batui Thrust reinterpreted as dextral strike-slip fault (Siuna Fault of Rusmana et al.)
Two-pulse tectonic uplift: (1) NW compression from N Banda Sea opening → Balantak Fault; (2) Late Pliocene NE push via Sorong Fault → Salodik Anticlinorium inversion
Siuna Valley is a transtensional rift (large strike-slip + normal) along Balantak Fault — Gulf of Poh opening since Late Miocene
ESO (East Sulawesi Ophiolite) obducted southward over Banggai-Sula in Late Neogene (not Early-Mid Miocene as previously thought)
Wekuli Fault = structural contact between ESO and Pompangeo metamorphic complex (both Cretaceous age)
Plio-Pleistocene coastal terraces at elevation up to 400 m asl; uplift rate ~1.84 mm/yr at 0.35 Ma reef terrace
Luwuk sinistral fault (NW-SE) and Poh dextral fault (NNW-SSE) form linked transtensional system with Biak block faults

properties.name: Batui/Salodik Thrust — NW-vergent thin-skinned
properties.full_name: Batui-Salodik Thrust — thin-skinned NW-vergent thrust-fold belt, Luwuk East Arm (Husein et al. 2014)
properties.revision_of: Previous thick-skinned SE-vergent model (Rusmana et al. 1993, Surono et al. 1993)
properties.source_apa: Husein et al. (2014). IPA 14-G-137, Proc. IPA 38th Conv.
properties.paper_finding: Thin-skinned NW-vergent thrust (basement uninvolved); forms central compressional compartment; key structure of Salodik fold-thrust belt formed during Late Pliocene inversion
properties.extraction_notes: Traza derivada de posición geográfica de Luwuk [122.79,-1.03] y Batui [122.63,-1.21] — localidades mencionadas en el texto. Trazo NW-SE aproximado siguiendo el frente de cabalgamiento.

conclusions.text: Batui/Salodik Thrust is thin-skinned (basement uninvolved) with NW vergence, not thick-skinned SE-vergent as previously modeled — fundamental revision of the Luwuk thrust-fold belt
conclusions.geodyn_implication: La vergencia NW del cinturón de Luwuk implica que la compresión viene del SE (avance de Banggai-Sula), no del NW — contrario a los modelos anteriores de frente orogénico hacia el SE

conclusions.text: Salodik fold-thrust belt formed during Late Pliocene NE-ward compression driven by Sorong Fault activation pushing Banggai-Sula
conclusions.geodyn_implication: Activación del Sorong Fault en el Plioceno Tardío como trigger de la deformación compresional en el brazo este de Sulawesi — vincula la tectónica regional (Sorong) con la deformación local (Batui Thrust)

properties.name: Balantak Fault — dextral strike-slip (Siuna Fault)
properties.full_name: Balantak Fault (= Siuna Fault sensu Rusmana 1993) — dextral strike-slip transtensional, East Arm Sulawesi
properties.tectonic_setting: Transtensional — large strike-slip with normal component; controls opening of Gulf of Poh (Siuna Valley)
properties.source_apa: Husein et al. (2014). IPA 14-G-137, Proc. IPA 38th Conv.
properties.paper_finding: Eastern segment of Batui Thrust reinterpreted as dextral strike-slip fault; Siuna Valley is transtensional rift opening Gulf of Poh since Late Miocene; NW-SE compression kinematic analysis from sheared gabbro at Pagimana
properties.extraction_notes: La Balantak Fault conecta hacia el SW con el frente del Batui Thrust en área de Batui [122.63,-1.21] y se extiende NE hacia el Balantak Peninsula. Posición derivada del texto y descripciones de afloramientos (Pagimana [122.03,-0.71], Siuna Village [~122.85,-0.82]).

conclusions.text: Balantak Fault is dextral strike-slip (not thrust as previously interpreted); controls transtensional Siuna Valley rift opening since Late Miocene
conclusions.geodyn_implication: La Balantak Fault forma el límite entre el compartimento extensional N (sobre ofiolita) y el compresional central — controla la tectónica de bloque del extremo NE del brazo este

conclusions.text: Kinematic analysis of sheared gabbro at Pagimana shows SE-NW compression and NE-SW extension — consistent with NE extension of Siuna rifting
conclusions.geodyn_implication: La ofiolita Balantak actúa como bloque rígido que partición la deformación entre thrust (SW) y transtensión (NE) durante el avance de Banggai-Sula

properties.name: Luwuk Sinistral Fault — NW-SE left-lateral
properties.full_name: Luwuk Sinistral Fault — NW-SE trending left-lateral strike-slip, Luwuk Town
properties.source_apa: Husein et al. (2014). IPA 14-G-137, Proc. IPA 38th Conv.
properties.paper_finding: Left-lateral strike-slip fault trending NW-SE south of Luwuk town; cuts Plio-Pleistocene reef terraces, Tertiary Poh Formation, Eo-Oligocene Salodik Formation and Paleogene ultramafics
properties.extraction_notes: Localizada al sur de Luwuk [122.79,-1.03]. Longitud y trazo cortos — localidad conocida pero traza exacta no coordinada en el texto.

conclusions.text: Luwuk sinistral fault (NW-SE) cross-cuts multiple formation ages up to Plio-Pleistocene — indicates recent activity
conclusions.geodyn_implication: Componente sinistral localizada en Luwuk Town — posible peligrosidad sísmica local en área urbana

properties.name: Wekuli Fault — ESO / Pompangeo contact
properties.full_name: Wekuli Fault — NNW-SSE structural contact East Sulawesi Ophiolite / Pompangeo Complex
properties.geological_contact: East Sulawesi Ophiolite (E) vs Pompangeo Metamorphic Complex (W) — both Cretaceous age
properties.source_apa: Husein et al. (2014). IPA 14-G-137, Proc. IPA 38th Conv.
properties.paper_finding: Wekuli Fault is NNW extension of Tomori Bay linear valley; marks ESO/Pompangeo contact; both Cretaceous age (Simandjuntak et al. 1997)
properties.extraction_notes: Posicionada como extensión NNW del Tomori Bay (Gulf of Tolo) hacia el norte, aproximadamente en la transición ESO-Pompangeo al oeste de Luwuk.

conclusions.text: Wekuli Fault separates ESO from Pompangeo complex — both Cretaceous; fault is structural contact, kinematics undetermined from this study
conclusions.geodyn_implication: La falla Wekuli marca el límite tectónico entre la ofiolita y el complejo metamórfico — potencial zona de discontinuidad sismogénica en profundidad

properties.name: Salodik Anticlinorium — NW-vergent inversion structure
properties.full_name: Salodik Anticlinorium — asymmetric anticline, NW-vergent, central compressional compartment, Luwuk
properties.fold_limbs: steep N forelimb, gentle S backlimb
properties.involves: Tertiary carbonates (Salodik Fm, Poh Fm) and Miocene clastics
properties.source_apa: Husein et al. (2014). IPA 14-G-137, Proc. IPA 38th Conv.
properties.paper_finding: Major anticlinorium with asymmetric profile (steep N, gentle S); S-shaped parasitic folds in northern limb indicate NW tectonic transport; formed by Late Pliocene inversion driven by Sorong Fault activation
properties.extraction_notes: Polígono amplio sobre el área de estudio de Luwuk. El anticlinorio comprende el dominio central entre Luwuk y Pagimana.

conclusions.text: Salodik Anticlinorium is Late Pliocene basin inversion structure driven by NE-ward compression via Sorong Fault — key structural trap for hydrocarbons (Tomori PSC gas field)
conclusions.geodyn_implication: La inversión del Plioceno Tardío en el brazo este es el resultado directo de la activación del Sistema Sorong — vincula la tectónica de gran escala (Sorong) con la formación de trampas de HC en el brazo este

properties.name: East Sulawesi Ophiolite (ESO) — brazo este Sulawesi
properties.full_name: East Sulawesi Ophiolite Complex — Late Cretaceous, obducted over Banggai-Sula in Late Neogene
properties.composition: highly sheared gabbro + basalt + magnesite veins (Pagimana outcrop)
properties.source_apa: Husein et al. (2014). IPA 14-G-137, Proc. IPA 38th Conv.
properties.paper_finding: ESO thrusted southward over Banggai-Sula in Late Neogene; Pagimana outcrop: highly sheared gabbro+basalt with magnesite veins along fault planes; initial inversion of Balantak Ophiolites at Late Miocene
properties.extraction_notes: Polígono regional del ESO sobre el brazo este de Sulawesi. Límite sur aproximado (frente de cabalgamiento). El ESO cubre la mayor parte del brazo este al norte del Batui Thrust.

conclusions.text: ESO obducted southward over Banggai-Sula in Late Neogene (Late Pliocene), not Middle Miocene as previously assumed — later timing revises the collision model
conclusions.geodyn_implication: La obducción tardía del ESO implica que la colisión Banggai-Sula con el brazo este es más joven (~5-6 Ma, consistent con literatura), con consecuencias para el ritmo de alzamiento y la madurez del sistema de HC

conclusions.text: Late Miocene initial inversion of Balantak Ophiolites; high-angle reverse fault contact ESO/Poh Formation observed in outcrop (Fig. 11)
conclusions.geodyn_implication: Dos fases de deformación en el ESO: inversión inicial Mioceno Tardío (Balantak Fault initiation) + compresión principal Plioceno Tardío (Sorong Fault) — complejo historial de deformación polifásico

properties.name: Perfil N-S — Luwuk a Pagimana
properties.full_name: Perfil geológico N-S transecting East Arm from Luwuk to Pagimana (Husein et al. 2014)
properties.source_apa: Husein et al. (2014). IPA 14-G-137, Proc. IPA 38th Conv.
properties.paper_finding: N-S and E-W transecting sections from field mapping document three structural compartments: N extensional (ophiolite), C compressional thrust-fold (carbonates), S gravitational (molasse)
properties.extraction_notes: Sección mencionada en metodología ('transecting north-south section and east-west section of Luwuk'). Traza aproximada Luwuk-Pagimana siguiendo el eje del estudio.

conclusions.text: Three structural compartments documented: N extensional (Balantak Ophiolite block-faulting), C compressional (Salodik Anticlinorium NW-vergent thrust), S gravitational (Kemumu rock slides)
conclusions.geodyn_implication: La compartimentación estructural del brazo este refleja la geometría compleja de la colisión con Banggai-Sula — distintos mecanismos en km de distancia
